mechanism_narrative: A higher 6-month growth rate in retail sales raises expected near-term corporate revenue and margins, so a positive RSAFS trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS snapshot before decision date was 436772.0
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month RSAFS trends are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 52% of walk-forward observations or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and risk appetite; when UNRATE rises quickly, S&P 500 60-day returns turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot before decision date was 5.4
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered average returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate liftoff from the zero lower bound raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the policy-rate crossing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% after being at or below 0.25% for at least 6 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot before decision date was 0.12
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS liftoff events produce non-negative mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or if negative-return hit rate after triggered events is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production indicates weaker goods output and operating leverage, lowering earnings expectations for cyclicals and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO snapshot before decision date was 105.1786
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of cases or if triggered returns are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with falling unemployment confirms broad labor-market strength, supporting household income and earnings revisions; the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot before decision date was 141367.0
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot before decision date was 5.4
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-falling condition does not outperform either single-series signal or if its 20-day positive-return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains near zero creates a reflation regime with low nominal discount rates and improving nominal revenues, predicting positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot before decision date was 235.74
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot before decision date was 0.12
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-reflation plus low-FEDFUNDS interaction fails to generate higher 60-day returns than CPI acceleration alone or if mean triggered returns are below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening construction demand, household confidence, and credit-sensitive activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST snapshot before decision date was 926.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if drawdown episodes do not underperform non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt CPI acceleration raises near-term inflation and policy-tightening risk, increasing equity discount rates and reducing valuation multiples; the signal predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot before decision date was 235.74
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of cases or if average triggered 5-day returns are not below unconditional returns.
